title: Leucanthemum arcticum
taxonomy: Leucanthemum arcticum is a member of the Asteraceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family includes over 23,000 species, making it one of the largest families of flowering plants. The Leucanthemum arcticum is further classified within the genus Leucanthemum, which contains several species of daisies and similar plants. The genus Leucanthemum is a part of the tribe Anthemideae, which is a sub-group of the Asteraceae family. The classification of Leucanthemum arcticum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Leucanthemum arcticum, also known as Arctic daisy, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies for its potential health benefits. Its flowers and leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Some studies suggest that extracts from this plant could have antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a potential natural remedy for skin infections. Further studies are necessary to confirm the effectiveness of Leucanthemum arcticum for treating various health conditions.

intro: Leucanthemum arcticum, also known as Arctic daisy, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum arcticum, particularly the flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, have been reported to possess antitumor, antiviral, and antibacterial activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Leucanthemum arcticum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
